SnowDistribution: Moist or humid snow throughout.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells later.  Breezy on the tops
Comment: Low confidence in forecasted snow amounts. Existing cornices expected to weaken. Falling ice from low altitude crags a potential objective hazard.
WeatherInfluences: Persistent snowfall near summits over the forecast period with rain at lower levels. Freezing at or just below summits. Strong East South East winds. Low confidence in forecasted snow amounts.
SnowStability: Stability will remain good in many places. Some new moist windslab is possible on a few West to North West aspects above 950m. Any new accumulations of less well bonded snow expected to be localised and confined to very steep locations. Elsewhere the snowpack will be moist but generally well stabilised. All existing cornices expected to weaken.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Some overnight showers of snow over summits with light rain at lower levels then largely dry during the day. Freezing near summits throughout. Light to moderate South Easterly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: Good stability in most places. Only limited drifting noted. Moist or humid snow depending on altitude. Older windslab on recent lee slopes remains well bonded.  The avalanche hazard is Low

SnowDistribution: Rain showers during the morning, clearing in the afternoon. Winds 10-20 mph. Variable firmness of snowpack.
Comment: A cloudy, wet day in prospect. Snow pack firmness is variable on the tops: some snow is firm, other areas are fairly soft underfoot.
WeatherInfluences: Easterly winds increasing to very strong by mid-day. Very light precipitation forecast, falling as sleety snow only on the highest tops. The freezing level will be on, or just above the summits.
SnowStability: The snowpack will remain well bonded and stable in all locations. Any new, moist snow deposits on the highest summits are not expected to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Light South-Easterly winds. Precipitation for most of the day, falling as sleety snow only on the highest tops. The freezing level was on or just above summit level.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded and stable throughout. A good cover of snow remains on most aspects above 700 metres with patchier cover below. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: No visibility throughout the day above 900 metres. Winds 20-25 mph. Moist snow at most levels, some wind exposed slopes firm and icy.
Comment: Slow consolidating trend, but with active windslab development above 1000 metres as further snow showers are driven in from the South-East.
WeatherInfluences: Precipitation is expected throughout the period, this falling initially as snow above 1000 metres, later above 900 metres. Winds will be South-Easterly strong overnight increasing to very strong during the day. The freezing level will drop to around 950 metres by dawn.
SnowStability: Fresh moderately bonded windslab will develop during the day on West through North-West to North-East aspects above 1000 metres. This will mostly accumulate in steep wind sheltered locations such as coire rims and gully tops. The existing snowpack will be subject to slow consolidation, but continues to present lingering weakness at depth. Slopes above 650 metres will present the greatest snow cover, with isolated patches below. Fresh cornices will develop. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Occasional snow showers above 1000 metres in the morning, followed by a mostly dry afternoon. Winds were fresh and South-South-Easterly. The freezing level was around 1100 metres at midday.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh moderately bonded windslab developed during the day on West through North-West to North-East aspects above 1000 metres. This was mostly confined to the coire rims, gully tops and other steep wind sheltered locations. Elsewhere the snowpack has been subject to slow consolidation. Below 650 metres gradual snow loss has resulted in limited cover characterised by snow patches in hollows and burn lines. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Generally wet snowpack at all elevations.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells.  Winds approx 10-20 mph.
Comment: Colder air from Tuesday evening on Easterly winds.
WeatherInfluences: Outbreaks of light rain and snow overnight and through the day with moderate to fresh East-South-East winds. Snow fall will be limited to the highest summits above around 1000 metres.
SnowStability: Accumulations of wet snow will form overnight and through the day on the highest summits, above around 1000 metres, with possibly some surface instabilities in steep locations around gully tops and coire rims. Elsewhere, the existing soft snowpack will continue to consolidate and will be stable, at lower elevations thawing and diminishing. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A mainly dry and bright day with isolated showers falling as rain and light snow above 950 metres. Light to moderate Southerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: There is a light covering of fresh moist and well bonded snow on a thin breakable crust on all aspects above 900 metres. Elsewhere the existing snowpack is soft and moist, thawing and diminishing at lower elevations. Stability is generally good. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Deep snow at all altitudes - very hard going on foot. Knee deep at 600m!  Cloud base was at 750 metres all day.  Winds 20 -30 mph progress difficult at times with stronger gusts.
Comment: Becoming slightly cooler, freezing level expected to be around 900 metres.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level is expected to drop to around 900 metres with precipitation falling as snow around this level by the end of the period. Winds will strengthen throughout the day, mainly South-Easterly, backing East-South-East by late afternoon.
SnowStability: New accumulations of windslab will build, mainly above 900 metres in wind sheltered locations on West to North aspects with South-West and North-East aspects also affected by cross-loading.  Where new accumulations develop over older snow-ice they will only be moderately bonded. Snow will be moist but well bonded at lower levels. Cornice stability will remain suspect. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: The freezing level was around 900 metres for most of the day. Precipitation was light, with occasional snow flurries falling above 800 metres. There was a strong breeze, mostly South-South East, occasionally South.
ObservedSnowStability: There has been some consolidation of recent deposits with snow loss noted at lower levels.The majority of the snow is soft and moist but instabilities persist in deeper deposits, mainly on South-West through West to North-East aspects above 1000 metres. Stability of any remaining cornices remains suspect. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Firm snow in places, breakable crust in others.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells.  Winds 20 -30 mph.
Comment: Rock and ice fall a hazard at lower and mid elevations where the temperature is above freezing.
WeatherInfluences: Showers throughout the period will turn to snow above around 950 metres by morning as the precipitation becomes heaviest, easing in the afternoon and turning to rain below 1100 metres. Strong East-South-Easterly winds.
SnowStability: The existing snowpack will remain well bonded and stable. Localised deposits of fresh unstable windslab will develop mainly on steep West to North aspects above 1150 metres. Windsheltered locations such as gullies and coire rims will be most affected. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Snow showers above around 950 metres early this morning cleared to give a mainly dry day with fresh South-Easterly winds. The freezing level was around 1000 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack is well bonded and stable. Shallow new snow deposits above around 950 metres are consolidating. The avalanche hazard is Low.
